perturbation.original_text: This NON-COMPETITION AGREEMENT AMENDMENT NO. 1 (this "Amendment") is entered into as of August 16, 2017, by and between VIVINT SOLAR, INC., a Delaware corporation (together with its successors and permitted assigns, "Vivint Solar"), and VIVINT, INC., a Utah corporation (together with its successors and permitted assigns "Vivint"). Each of Vivint Solar and Vivint may also be referred to herein individually as a "Party", and collectively as the "Parties".
perturbation.changed_text: This NON-COMPETITION AGREEMENT AMENDMENT NO. 1 (this "Amendment") is entered into as of August 16, 2017, by and between VIVINT SOLAR, INC., a Delaware corporation (together with its successors and permitted assigns, "Vivint Solar"), and VIVINT, INC., a Utah corporation (together with its successors and permitted assigns "Vivint"). Each of Vivint Solar and Vivint may also be referred to herein individually as a "Signatory", and collectively as the "Signatories".
perturbation.explanation: The term 'Party' is being replaced with 'Signatory'. This creates confusion because the agreement consistently uses 'Party' and 'Parties' later on. Changing this fundamental definition introduces ambiguity about which obligations apply to each entity. Specifically in the rest of the document where 'Parties' is used can now be contradicted.
perturbation.contradicted_text: RECITALS WHEREAS, Vivint Solar and Vivint are affiliate business entities, under the common control and ownership of 313 Acquisition, LLC, a Delaware limited liability company. WHEREAS, the Parties had entered into a Non-Competition Agreement dated September 30, 2014, by and between the Parties (collectively, the "Non-Competition Agreement") to set out certain restrictive covenants of each Party. WHEREAS, the Parties wish to amend the existing obligations under the Non-Competition Agreement. WHEREAS, the Parties also desire to extend the term of the non-solicitation obligations under the Non-Competition Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'Party' with 'Signatory'. The contradicted text repeatedly uses 'Parties' to define the entities' roles and obligations ('WHEREAS, the Parties had entered into...by and between the Parties'). Replacing 'Party' with 'Signatory' creates a direct contradiction because the contradicted section relies on the original definition of 'Parties' to establish obligations. This misalignment in terminology, as defined in the 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' perturbation type, introduces ambiguity and a strong likelihood of legal dispute over which entities are bound by the agreement.

perturbation.original_text: This Amendment will apply and be effective only with respect to the provisions of the Non- Competition Agreement specifically referred to herein. Except as otherwise set forth in this Amendment, the Non-Competition Agreement will continue in full force and effect in accordance with its terms.
perturbation.changed_text: This Amendment will apply and be effective only with respect to the provisions of the Non- Competition Agreement specifically referred to herein. Except as otherwise set forth in this Amendment, the Original Agreement will continue in full force and effect in accordance with its terms.
perturbation.explanation: Replacing 'Non-Competition Agreement' with 'Original Agreement' creates confusion. While arguably referring to the same thing, it's undefined. Now the term 'Non-Competition Agreement' which is used to define other terms later on will now become ambiguous and unclear where it refers to.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1. Definitions. Any capitalized term used but not defined in this Amendment will have the meaning set forth for that term in the Non-Competition Agreement or the Master Framework Agreement, dated September 30, 2016, by and between the Parties (the "Master Framework Agreement").
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Non-Competition Agreement' to 'Original Agreement' creates ambiguity because the contradicted text relies on a specific definition of 'Non-Competition Agreement' for undefined terms. By introducing 'Original Agreement', which is not defined within the amendment or explicitly linked to the Non-Competition Agreement, it's unclear which agreement's definitions apply, thus contradicting the contradicted text that states, "Any capitalized term used but not defined in this Amendment will have the meaning set forth for that term in the Non-Competition Agreement..."

perturbation.original_text: Non-Solicitation. Section 4 of the Non-Competition Agreement is hereby deleted in its entirety and replaced with the following: "Term. This Agreement will become effective on the Effective Date, and will continue until the expiration of the "Sales Term" as that term is defined in the Sales Dealer Agreement dated as of August 16, 2017 between Vivint and Vivint Solar Developer, LLC (the "Term")."
perturbation.changed_text: Non-Solicitation. Section 4 of the Non-Competition Agreement is hereby deleted in its entirety and replaced with the following: "Term. This Amendment will become effective on the Effective Date, and will continue until the expiration of the "Sales Term" as that term is defined in the Sales Dealer Agreement dated as of August 16, 2017 between Vivint and Vivint Solar Developer, LLC (the "Period")."
perturbation.explanation: Changing the word 'Term' to 'Period' within the nested definition causes contradiction. The section defines the end date according to the end of the 'Sales Term', but then renames the 'Term' as 'Period'. If the agreement references the previous definition of 'Term' elsewhere, there will be contradiction. What 'Term' in (the "Term"). section 3, is reffering to
perturbation.contradicted_text: Section 4 of the Non-Competition Agreement is hereby deleted in its entirety and replaced with the following: "Term. This Amendment will become effective on the Effective Date, and will continue until the expiration of the "Sales Term" as that term is defined in the Sales Dealer Agreement dated as of August 16, 2017 between Vivint and Vivint Solar Developer, LLC (the "Period")."
perturbation.justification: The changed text renames the 'Term' to 'Period' within the definition. The contradicted section references 'Term', but the modified text replaces 'Term' with 'Period', creating a misalignment in terminology. This contradicts the agreement because it is unclear what 'Term' is referring to. This misaligned terminology leads to an in-text contradiction because the contract uses multiple terms interchangeably without defining them, leading to conflicting obligations.
